req.title: Submit leave request — variant 4
req.statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a leave-request submission flow with four distinct concern areas: (1) valid submissions within 1-30 business days, (2) boundary at exactly 30 days, (3) invalid date ordering, (4) overlapping leave detection, and (5) requests exceeding 30 days without hr-admin role. I additionally cover rate-limiting as a negative path. Heuristics applied: EP for the normal valid case, BVA for the 30-day boundary, NEG for invalid date order and overlap, DT for the >30-day approval-gate rule, and STC for the rate-limit state transition.

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_valid_within_30_days
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Target date range spans 10 business days
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with start_date=next Monday, end_date=+10 business days, type=annual
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_valid_within_30_days(auth_client, clean_leave_state, next_business_day, assert_email_sent):
    start = next_business_day(offset=1)
    end = next_business_day(offset=10)
    payload = {"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "leave_type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "request_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "pending"
    assert_email_sent(to=auth_client.user_email, subject_contains="Leave Request")
    assert <notification_store>.manager_notified(body["request_id"])

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exactly_30_business_days
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Target date range spans exactly 30 business days
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with start_date=next Monday, end_date=+30 business days, type=annual
expected: HTTP 201 Created without requiring hr-admin approval
Response body does not contain hr_approval_required flag set to true
trace: R-WEB-060: between 1 and 30 business days — upper boundary
R-WEB-060: requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval (30 is inclusive, no escalation)

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_start_after_end_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
expected: No leave record is created in the database
trace: R-WEB-060: error pathway — 400 when start_date > end_date

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
An approved leave record already exists for the target date range
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with date range overlapping the existing approved leave
trace: R-WEB-060: error pathway — 409 when overlapping existing leave
tests.executable:
def test_submit_leave_request_overlapping_existing_returns_409(auth_client, create_leave_record, next_business_day):
    start = next_business_day(offset=1)
    end = next_business_day(offset=5)
    create_leave_record(user_id=auth_client.user_id, start=start, end=end, status="approved")

    overlap_start = next_business_day(offset=3)
    overlap_end = next_business_day(offset=7)
    payload = {"start_date": overlap_start, "end_date": overlap_end, "leave_type": "annual"}

    resp = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "OVERLAPPING_LEAVE"

tests.name: test_submit_leave_request_exceeds_30_days_without_hr_admin_returns_422
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
User does NOT have hr-admin role
No existing leave overlaps the target date range
Target date range spans 31 business days
actions: POST /leave-requests/new with start_date=next Monday, end_date=+31 business days, type=annual
trace: R-WEB-060: requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval
R-WEB-060: error pathway — 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

tests.name: test_leave_request_rate_limit_triggers_after_threshold
preconditions: User is authenticated as a full-time employee
Rate-limit counter for the user is at threshold (state: saturated)
actions: Submit N+1 leave requests in rapid succession where N equals the per-user rate limit
expected: N+1 request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
trace: R-WEB-060: operation must be rate-limited per user
tests.executable:
def test_leave_request_rate_limit_triggers_after_threshold(auth_client, clean_leave_state, next_business_day, business_days_end, rate_limit_config):
    limit = rate_limit_config("leave_requests_per_window")
    responses = []
    for i in range(limit):
        start = next_business_day(offset=1 + i * 2)
        end = business_days_end(start, count=1)
        r = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json={"start_date": start, "end_date": end, "leave_type": "annual"})
        responses.append(r)

    assert all(r.status_code == 201 for r in responses), "Expected all requests within limit to succeed"

    start_extra = next_business_day(offset=1 + limit * 2)
    end_extra = business_days_end(start_extra, count=1)
    final = auth_client.post("/leave-requests/new", json={"start_date": start_extra, "end_date": end_extra, "leave_type": "annual"})

    assert final.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in final.headers
